en.extract:
The Pemba white-eye (Zosterops vaughani) is a species of bird in the family Zosteropidae. It is endemic to Tanzania.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests, subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, subtropical or tropical mangrove forests, and dry savanna.


History
The Pemba white-eye derives its scientific name, Zosterops vaughani, from John Henry Vaughan, after whom it was named.


References

The '''Pemba white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops vaughani''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Zosteropidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Tanzania]].

Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical dry [[forest]]s, subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, subtropical or tropical mangrove forests, and dry [[savanna]].

==History==
The Pemba white-eye derives its scientific name, ''Zosterops vaughani'', from [[John Henry Vaughan]], after whom it was named.<ref>{{cite journal | editor = Nancy M Taylor | year =1924 | title =Zosterops vaughani | journal = Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club | volume =44 | page =41 | publisher = Clarendon Press | location =Oxford | url=https://archive.org/details/bulletinofbritis44tayl | access-date =2014-08-20 | quote = Named in honour of Mr. John H. Vaughan, of the Colonial Civil Service, who has very kindly presented the type to the National Collection.}}</ref>
